power_outage: Utility power sags, UPS demand ramps up, critical loads ride through a brief transfer dip, then the plant stabilizes on backup power.
cooling_degradation: One cooling train loses capacity, the standby unit ramps up, room temperatures drift higher, and IT load starts to warm unevenly.
load_transfer: A planned electrical transfer shifts load between redundant paths with a brief inrush on the receiving side before settling into a new steady state.
demand_response: A utility price spike triggers policy-based GPU load shedding, lowers compute power draw, and lets the cooling loop recover before workloads ramp back toward normal.
